L2.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where the XML parser used during configuration initialization does not restrict entity reference expansion. An attacker who can influence the XML configuration input can leverage external entity declarations to read arbitrary files from the server or perform server-side request forgery.

L3.area: XML configuration file parsing and entity resolution within the connection pool configuration subsystem
L3.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where the XML parser used to read connection pool XML configuration files does not disable entity reference expansion. An attacker who can control or influence the XML configuration input can include external entity declarations, enabling file disclosure or server-side request forgery during the connection pool initialization process.

ground_truth.reason: The XML configuration parser creates a DocumentBuilderFactory without disabling entity reference expansion, allowing XXE attacks via crafted XML configuration files during initialization.
